title: Set Bit
hints: Start with 0 and set the A-th and B-th bits.
Use bitwise OR to set bits: 0 | (1 << A) | (1 << B).
If A and B are the same, you only need to set one bit: 0 | (1 << A).
The expression (1 << A) creates a mask with only the A-th bit set.
Combine both masks using OR to set both bits at once.
